name: Cornish Game Hens with Peach Glaze
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup table salt (or 2/3 cup Kosher salt)
salt, pepper, and spices -- to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Brine the hens: Dissolve the salt in about 2 quarts cold water in a small, clean bucket or large bowl. Add the hens breast side down; refrigerate 2 to 3 hours. Remove, rinse thoroughly, pat dry, and prick skin all over with point of paring knife.
2. Adjust oven rack to lower-middle position. Preheat oven to 400 degrees.
3. Butterfly the hens: Using kitchen shears, remove the backbone and flatten the birds breast side up with palm of hand.
4. Loosen the skin around leg/thighs of hens and along breastbone to expose meat. Generously sprinkle exposed meat with salt, pepper, and herb of choice.
5. Melt margarine in a small bowl; stir in peach preserves Apricot or other preserves can be used. Add spices (white pepper and basil).
6. Rub the hens with about 2 tablespoons softened margarine.
7. Brush hens with margarine and preserves mixture.
8. Place hens on a rack over a roasting pan or jelly roll pan; roast until nicely browned, 20 to 25 minutes (30 minutes is ideal). Turn oven from bake to broil setting and broil 5 minutes more for additional browning.
9. Serve with the remaining glaze drizzled over each hen, if desired.
Use peach preserves or other fruit preserve of choice.
